intro: Campylotropis muehleana, also known as Muehlena's Campylotropis, is a perennial legume native to the mountainous regions of China and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Campylotropis muehleana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Campylotropis muehleana is often prepared into teas, decoctions, and infusions to take advantage of its health benefits, and it is also used in the production of herbal supplements and tinctures.
